headline: Only 3 of 13 state assemblies publish hansard online
context: A TFA audit found that only Selangor, Penang, and Sarawak publish complete state assembly proceedings online, leaving 10 states with no accessible legislative record.

cards.big: 10 out of 13 Malaysian states keep assembly debates secret
cards.sub: Citizens cannot read what their elected representatives said or voted on

cards.big: Article 10 guarantees free speech but no law mandates hansard publication
cards.sub: State assemblies have full discretion over transparency of their own proceedings

cards.big: Selangor's online hansard system cost only RM240,000 to implement
cards.sub: Budget is not a credible barrier — it equals 0.001% of most state budgets

cards.big: Johor and Perak last published accessible hansard records in 2019
cards.sub: Five-year gap means an entire state election cycle passed without public record

cards.big: Democracy without records is performance — accountability requires a transcript
Should federal law mandate all 13 states publish assembly proceedings?
